title: Exploring the Environmental Costs of AI
description: On Thursday, November 22nd, 2024, the Institute for Public Knowledge hosted a discussion with Benedetta Brevini, Nicole Starosielski, Sanjana Paul, and Tamara Kneese. They discussed the environmental harm that is posed by AI.

title: XYZ Paris 2024 - Assessing the Environmental Impact of Generative AI: Challenges & Opportunities
description: Assessing the Environmental Impact of Generative AI: Challenges & Opportunities.

title: A Simple Guide to Everyday Climate Action
description: In her TEDx talk, Tamma shares how her journey from South Africa to the UK shaped her deep understanding of sustainability and the climate crisis, emphasizing the urgent need to reduce consumption to protect Earth's interconnected ecosystems.

title: Implications of Artificial Intelligence-Related Data Center Electricity Use and Emissions: A Workshop
description: The National Academies hosted a two-day workshop bringing together experts from industry, utilities, and government to examine AI data centers' electricity usage, exploring its energy demands, mitigation strategies, regional siting considerations, and renewable resource availability.

title: Stanford Energy Seminar: AI & Climate Change Mitigation
description: The second edition of the Artificial Intelligence for Climate Change Mitigation Roadmap (November 2024) examines AI's role in addressing the climate crisis, identifying key sectors where AI can have the greatest impact, and weighing the potential emissions reductions against the increased power demand from AI systems, with a discussion led by the report's authors.

title: Behind the Algorithmic Code: The Environmental Footprint of AI's Supply Chains
description: Dr. Ana Valdivia’s 18-month investigation, funded by a British Academy grant, explores the supply chains of AI in Latin America, introducing the concept of supply chain capitalism of AI to analyze the minerals, commodities, and facilities essential to AI development, while examining the political and environmental controversies surrounding data centers through a transdisciplinary approach.

title: Making Cloud Sustainability Actionable with FinOps
description: Mike Jaco and Vik Saluja of Mastercard give FinOps X 2024 a look at how Mastercard gathers and analyzes its cloud carbon-focused cost and usage data, and what actions they believe practitioners should take to better inform leadership of cloud sustainability issues.

title: Green Tech Talk: Leaders on UX Design, Green Software, AI & Big Data
description: Join 🌍 Green Tech Exposed to explore innovative solutions and technologies driving a greener future 🌱.

title: AI in Transportation: Trends, Impact, and Sustainability
description: Join Adnan Kordab, Head of Business Technologies at Emircom, and Kunal Aman, Director of Marketing at SAS, as they explore the latest AI trends revolutionizing transportation by enhancing efficiency, sustainability, and climate impact through cutting-edge technology.

title: IEEE Online Forum on Climate Change Technologies (OFCCT)
description: Join the conversation on the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) forum on climate change technologies.

title: AI and Sustainable Impact
description: In this webinar, Harnessing Knowledge for AI and Sustainable Impact, Nobre, F.S. (2024) explores the intersection of sustainability, innovation, and entrepreneurship in the age of artificial intelligence, discussing how AI-driven knowledge can drive sustainable progress, as presented at the PPGADM extension event at the Federal University of Paraná on November 26, 2024.

title: AI for Coastal Living
description: 🌊 Join GDG Brunswick for a captivating journey into Artificial Intelligence and its potential to revolutionize coastal living! 🎉 This recording of the second half of our "AI for Coastal Living" event features expert insights, interactive demos, and engaging discussions on how AI can address real-world challenges faced by coastal communities.

title: AI for Climate
description: In addition to its potentially game-changing role in advancing energy technologies, AI could be similarly poised to support breakthrough innovations in climate science. Speakers will discuss the opportunities and challenges of using AI to address questions in climate research.

title: AI Solutions for Climate Action and Insights from COP28
description: In this episode we are discussing about climate change solutions, the impact that technology has involving green finance with Bjorn-Soren Gigler PhD, renowned Head of Data Economy, Digital and Green Twin Transition.

title: Professor Phoebe Koundouri: AI Analysis of Global-Regional Policy Framework Sustainability
description: Professor Phoebe Koundouri spoke on 'AI Analysis of Global-Regional Policy Framework Sustainability Transition Reporting' at this year's World Sustainability Conference.

title: Growing Impact: Feeding a Changing World
description: PlantVillage leverages smartphone technology and machine learning to empower smallholder farmers, who produce a significant share of the world’s food, by providing innovative solutions to agricultural challenges, with a global expansion strategy aimed at mitigating the impacts of climate change.

title: Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning
description: Lecture seminars on multiple topics ranging from forecasting better weather patterns to enhanced cooling sustainability operations in data centers.

title: Responsible AI: The Impact of AI on the Environment
description: Join us for a presentation on responsible AI and clean technology. We will cover ethical principles guiding AI development, innovations in energy efficiency, sustainable data practices, and the importance of governance, while highlighting real-world case studies and future trends.

title: Smart City, Smarter Citizens with Mayor San-Cheng Chang
description: Join us for a discussion on how AI can be used to improve smart city planning processes and ultimately build smarter cities.

title: From Policy to Practice: The Path Forward for U.S. Energy with Julia Pyper
description: Join us for a crystal-ball discussion on how the federal government can participate in the energy transition process.

title: Nature as Technology with Anna Lerner
description: In this episode of the ReFi Podcast, Anna Lerner, CEO of the Climate Collective, explores how AI and blockchain can enhance environmental accountability, the importance of product market fit for climate tech entrepreneurs, and the need to redirect harmful subsidies toward climate and biodiversity goals while emphasizing local community engagement in climate initiatives.

title: COP29 and Climate Change: Creating AI that Works for All
description: Tune in now as Dr. Ajay Mathur discusses various aspects of AI for climate action. He also explores whether AI can serve as a tool for all or if it will widen existing climate inequities

title: GenAI Series - Towards Environmental Responsible GenAI: Carbon, Water and Beyond
description: Tune in with Pengfei as we discuss the environmental costs of GenAI adoption in terms of carbon, water, and other aspects including public health with the NCAR Computational and Information Systems Laboratory (CISL).

title: AI For Nature: From Science to Impact
description: How can AI help protect biodiversity and combat the alarming loss of species worldwide? At H2O GenAI World NY 2024, Tanya Berger-Wolf, a leader in AI for conservation, delivers a powerful keynote on the intersection of technology and ecology.

title: Can AI Help Climate Change?
description: In this lightboard video, Stacey Gifford, a Chemist with IBM Research, explains how we can use artificial intelligence (AI) to develop new materials to help address one of the biggest challenges we face today, climate change.

title: How The Massive Power Draw Of Generative AI Is Overtaxing Our Grid
description: With over 8,000 data centers worldwide, the energy demands of generative AI are outpacing infrastructure capacity, as AI queries consume significantly more power than traditional searches, and training large models generates substantial CO₂ and water use; in response, companies like Vantage are building data centers closer to power sources while the industry explores alternative energy and grid resilience solutions.

title: How to Spot Greenwashing & Monitor Nature 24/7
description: Mike Odenhoven, co-founder of Sumthing, discusses with Jennifer how companies can distinguish genuine sustainability efforts from greenwashing, while also highlighting Sumthing’s current and future use of AI, emphasizing its potential to monitor environmental data such as continuous forest sounds beyond human capability.

title: Why Nuclear Energy is Suddenly Making a Comeback
description: In the 2010s, US nuclear plants were struggling to compete against cheap natural gas and renewable energy sources. But the intensifying threat of climate change and the rise of AI have changed the conversation. To bridge the gap to a carbon-free future, America is debating whether or not to build out its atomic power industry.

title: AI for Animals! Earth Species Project Featured
description: Katie Zacarian is the CEO and Co-Founder of Earth Species Project, a non-profit dedicated to using AI to decode animal communication to find new ways of listening to and learning from the rest of nature.  She discusses the incredible potential of AI in interspecies communication.

title: The Tipping Points of Climate Change — And Where We Stand
description: In this TED talk, Centre co-founder Johan Rockström runs you through the latest in climate science, tipping points, and why he is both nervous but also still optimistic about the current state of the planet.

title: New Energy Systems: Turning Challenges into Opportunities for Sustainable
description: Emre Gençer, Co-founder & CEO of Sesame Sustainability, explores innovative strategies for transforming energy systems, including optimizing renewable integration and deep decarbonization, showcasing how real-world data and modeling can help redesign infrastructure to balance emissions, cost, and reliability for a sustainable future.

title: How AI Causes Serious Environmental Problems (But Might Also Provide Solutions)
description: Companies and the academic community are rushing to take advantage of big, recent advances in artificial intelligence. In many cases, they're using AI to minimize humanity's impact on the environment and find solutions for the climate crisis. But AI consumes an enormous amount of natural resources, like water and electricity, in order to function. So, is AI a climate solution or problem?

title: Interview with Dr. Sasha Luccioni, AI and Climate Lead at Hugging Face
description: Washington AI Network founder Tammy Haddad interviews Dr. Sasha Luccioni, AI and climate lead at Hugging Face and a 2024 Time100 Most Influential People in AI honoree. The interview was recorded at the Élysée Palace in Paris during the No AI Without Women conference. Luccioni discusses the environmental challenges linked to AI, along with strategies for developing and deploying AI solutions to spur climate action and encourage long-term sustainability.

title: Session One: AI Environmental Footprint: Development and Usage
description: What is the environmental impact of the training and widespread deployment of numerous large language models and other AI systems? What are the trends developing, and what can we anticipate for the next 5-10 years? What might organizations, governments, and individuals do in response?

title: Session Two: AI and Water Usage
description: What do we know about the water usage required by AI tools? What are the environmental justice implications of this usage? How are AI developers and other stakeholders addressing this key challenge? How are impacted communities responding?

title: Session Three: Greening AI
description: What are some current efforts to make AI more environmentally friendly? Are there corporations or other organizations that might serve as models in this context? How does the amount of research and investment in this area compare to the attention and funding focused on other AI-related efforts? What trends do you see in this space, and are there promising developments we should keep an eye on?

title: Session Four: AI for Sustainability
description: What are some ways in which AI is currently being used to support environmental protection goals and to promote environmental justice? Do the benefits of such efforts outweigh the harms? Can that balance be improved even further? What are some promising developments and areas yet to be explored related to AI for sustainability?

title: Session Five: AI and the Environment: Business Implications
description: How does the environmental impact of AI, in all its complexity, intersect with business ethics and economic concerns, and with various corporations’ efforts related to environmental, social, and governance issues? What duties to AI developers owe to shareholders, employees, and members of the communities in which they operate? Who are the people best positioned to help shape related business practices, and how might they do that?

title: Session Six: AI, Regulation, and Environmental Law
description: What role does current environmental law play in addressing the environmental impacts of AI development and deployment? What are some new laws and regulations proposed in response to those impacts so far? What should we be demanding of our representatives and regulators?

title: Davos 25: The IT POV with Ralph Loura
description: Welcome Davos 25 -- The IT POV. We are gearing up to attend the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland January 20, with SustainableIT's delegation of information technology leaders and innovators. We are checking in prior to the event to heard their goals and expectations. In this episode, hear from SustainableIT.org's Board Chair, Ralph Loura.

title: Ionics for Energy-Efficient AI Hardware
description: As AI's energy demand and CO₂ emissions rise unsustainably, this talk explores how physical neural networks, which integrate processing and memory, can dramatically improve computing efficiency, introducing ionic synaptic devices that mimic biological synapses to achieve ultra-low energy, high-speed, and reliable performance, paving the way for next-generation AI chips and bio-inspired neural networks.

title: How Google's DeepMind is Using AI to Tackle Climate Change
description: AI is often seen as a magical fix-all. It's not, but it is, says Sims Witherspoon, a powerful tool for unlocking communities' problem-solving capacities. Witherspoon is a program manager at Google's DeepMind, which made headlines when it reduced energy required to cool Google Data Centres by 40 per cent. Energy consumption is one of the problems AI can be used for, and DeepMind is working on it.

title: ADSW 2025: Connecting Innovation to Sustainable Progress
description: In this special episode for Abu Dhabi Sustainability Week (ADSW) 2025, Lauren Woodman, CEO of DataKind, discusses the potential of AI and data science to address some of the most pressing challenges of the past 50 years while unlocking new opportunities for socio-economic growth. At ADSW 2025, AI takes center stage, with talks exploring its transformative potential to fast-track the transition to sustainable growth.

title: IGHS60 - AI, Data Governance, and the Future: Insights from Box CEO Aaron Levie
description: Jim Merrifield interviews Aaron Levie, CEO of Box, about the evolution of Box, the role of AI in data management, and the importance of information governance, highlighting how organizations must adapt their data architectures, the rise of AI agents for specific roles, and the ethical considerations of AI deployment, while emphasizing the need for structured data to maximize AI's potential.

title: Applying Indigenous Wisdom to Create Sustainable Tech
description: Keolu Fox, the first Native Hawaiian to earn a Ph.D in genome sciences and an advisor to Variant Bio, shares his visionary approach to technology inspired by Indigenous knowledge systems like the Hawaiian ahupua‘a land management system. In this talk, he highlights the wisdom of circular, balanced systems perfected over generations and applies these principles to modern challenges like genome data sharing, sustainable data centers, and Earth-friendly computation.

title: Conservation & Tech
description: Discover inspiring talks from past DLD Conferences and catch the highlights on the this channel. Stay updated with thought leaders, groundbreaking ideas, and key moments from our events!

title: Green Manufacturing and Supply Chains and the Role of Green IT
description: In this CXO Bytes episode, Sanjay Podder and May Yap, SVP & CIO of Jabil, discuss the intersection of green IT, responsible AI, and sustainable manufacturing, highlighting Jabil’s integration of renewable energy, circular economy principles, and AI-driven solutions into its operations, its commitment to carbon neutrality by 2045, and key initiatives like energy-efficient manufacturing, water conservation, and e-waste management, while emphasizing the role of green IT practices and AI in driving sustainable innovation.

title: Space Tech for Smart Cities: SatSure and KaleidEO
description: The key to solving urban challenges might just be up above the world—in the satellites. From enabling smarter farming to tackling climate change, Satsure and Kaleido are reshaping how we understand and build cities through space tech and remote sensing.

title: SSL4EO-2024 Summer School Recordings
description: Recordings from the 2024 summer school "SSL4EO: Self-Supervised Learning for Earth Observation" in Copenhagen.

title: Did ChatGPT Really Cause the Los Angeles Wildfires?
description: Los Angeles is battling devastating wildfires, with 25 lives lost and millions under threat. Amidst the chaos, social media has found an unusual scapegoat: ChatGPT. Is AI really to blame? Did AI’s environmental impact truly play a role in this crisis? Palki Sharma tells you.

title: Sustainable Data Centres: Practical Examples Of How Open Source Technologies Helps On The Journey To Sustainability
description: Sustainable Data Centres: Practical examples of how open source technologies helps on the journey to sustainability - Jon Laban, European Director, Open Compute Project Foundation

title: Delivering Hybrid IT Solutions  with Ensono
description: How are you finding, within the ecosystem, the data center operators? Are people recognizing that they need to upgrade or migrate, are they feeling pressure?

title: AI in Energy: Driving Efficiency and Sustainability
description: Sriram Sundar, VP and Business Head of Energy Core at Infosys, joins Jeff Kavanaugh to discuss AI’s transformative role in the energy sector, highlighting how digital twins, advanced analytics, and intelligent assets optimize refinery yields, boost productivity, and support sustainable energy transitions, ultimately unlocking new opportunities for a cleaner and more efficient energy future.

title: Navigating Tech with Hannah Hyatt: UX, AI, and Sustainability
description: Hannah Hyatt, Senior UX Researcher at Dell, joins Alyson Freeman to discuss the intersections of UX research, AI, and sustainability, sharing her journey from anthropology to tech, her advocacy for women in STEM, and insights into the environmental costs of cloud computing, ethical AI use, and accessibility innovations through generative AI, while encouraging aspiring researchers to own their brilliance, build networks, and embrace collaboration.

title: Episode 2: Data Centers, Smart Building, Infrastructure & Power Generation
description: In this episode, Emmanuel Daniel and Dr. Yongchoon Park explore the transformation of buildings and infrastructure through AI and data-driven systems, discussing the evolution of intelligent ecosystems, power challenges in data centers, grid resiliency, and onsite power generation, while highlighting the paradox of digital services versus sustainability and envisioning a future where power generation and consumption coexist harmoniously through diverse energy sources.

title: The Future of AI in Sustainability Panel at Net Zero LIVE London 2024
description: N/A

title: Challenging AI with Geertrui Mieke de Ketelaere
description: Geertrui Mieke de Ketelaere joins Kimberly to explore the uncertain trajectory of AI, discussing its social and environmental sustainability, the impact of humanlike AI on social anxiety, challenges with open-source AI, public literacy and regulation, and the Safe AI Companion Collective, while using the matryoshka doll analogy to unpack AI systems and emphasizing the need to expand perspectives beyond human intelligence to ensure we become good ancestors in AI development and use.

title: The Environmental Impact of Your Chatbot Habit - Why it Might Be Time to Say Bye to the AI
description: To help us understand the real cost of all our ChatGPT queries and Dall-E diversions, we’re talking to two women who are both fighting for more transparency around these very questions. Dr. Sasha Luccioni, the Climate Lead at AI company Hugging Face, has chosen to fight for more sustainable AI from within an AI company. On the other hand, Yoca Arditi-Rocha, executive director at the CLEO Institute is deeply skeptical that businesses will choose to put the planet above profit on their own accord.

title: The Digital Revolution: Agriculture, AI, and Climate Change
description: In this episode of The Upper Deck, Sardar Xin Khan sits down with Tabbish Mahmood, a visionary leader in Technology, Digital Transformation, and AgriTech. With a deep understanding of how innovation shapes industries, Tabbish shares insights on the intersection of AI, agriculture, and climate change.

title: How Google, Microsoft And Amazon Are Racing To Solve The AI Energy Crisis
description: As AI-driven data center power demand skyrockets, Big Tech is investing heavily in innovative energy solutions, including hydrogen, small nuclear reactors, fusion, and geothermal, with major backing from OpenAI’s Sam Altman, Microsoft, Google, and Amazon, while China outspends the U.S. in some areas and DeepSeek's efficiency breakthroughs raise questions about future energy needs; CNBC goes behind the scenes with energy start-ups to explore whether the AI power crisis could accelerate the long-awaited transition to low-carbon, scalable, and affordable energy.

title: Securing Our Water Future: How to Finance and Innovate for Impact
description: A webinar hosted by TTI Europe & France and Water Unite featured expert insights on addressing the global water crisis, highlighting the importance of partnerships (Satya Tripathi), disruptive investment models (Cyrille Antignac), and AI-powered agriculture (Amira Cheniour) as key solutions for water security.

title: Data Center Protest Groups with Ashley Studholme and Julie Bolthouse
description: Speakers from the Prince William Conservation Alliance and Piedmont Environmental Council speak with DCD's Matt Gooding about the impact of data centers on communities.

title: AI as a Top Energy User: Is It Sustainable? | IIA Davos 2025
description: Join us at World Forum 2025 for a dynamic panel on AI’s energy demands and environmental impact, where experts explore sustainable data centers, renewable solutions, and policies shaping a greener future.

title: "Artificial Intelligence: Decoded" on the BBC News
description: Dr Stephanie Hare reports for BBC News "Artificial Intelligence: Decoded" on how artists and creatives in France are using AI.

title: Big Tech's Big Bet on Nuclear Power
description: Faced with increasing demand for energy to fuel artificial intelligence, and the need to cut down on carbon emissions, companies like Microsoft, Google and Amazon have announced major investments in nuclear power.

title: The Hidden Environmental Cost of AI
description: Everyone is talking about AI, and rightly so! AI can potentially solve many of our most complex problems. It is finding use across industries and sectors. But all this comes at a cost. And surprisingly, not many are talking about it. AI has a huge negative environmental impact.

title: From Chaos to Code: How AI Can Tame the Climate Crisis | Columbia AI Summit
description: This panel will detail how AI is emerging as a powerful ally in supporting disaster preparedness and building resilience across interconnected systems.

title: UNU AI Network: Restoring & Re-Wilding Local Wildlife Habitats With Autonomous Drones Using AI
description: Presentation by Prof. Markus Krebsz at the UNU AI Network's Dialogue for Action session with topics on "Nature and AI".

title: The Hidden Costs of AI: Unpacking Its Energy and Water Footprint
description: As AI continues to reshape industries and our daily lives, its environmental impact is an increasingly important consideration. While much focus has been placed on AI’s energy consumption, its impact on other vital resources, such as water, is often overlooked.

title: America Wastes $6+ Billion Worth Of Recyclables A Year. Can Robots And AI Help? | AI In Action
description: The United States throws away $6.5 billion worth of reusable material every year, and the recycling rate has remained flat for over a decade. Now, tech companies are using artificial intelligence and robotics to make the process safer, more efficient, and more common. But can they really raise the recycling rate in a country with more than 9,000 recycling programs?

title: How China Could Beat The U.S. To Nuclear Fusion, As AI Power Needs Surge
description: Despite decades of U.S. leadership in nuclear fusion, China is now spending twice as much and building projects faster to beat the U.S. to commercial fusion power. The elusive, limitless, clean energy source has seen a rapid influx of private funds as AI power demands surge, with billions from the likes of OpenAI's Sam Altman, Microsoft and Google. But Satellite images show China is rapidly building giant new fusion projects, while cornering the supply chain and talent pool to get there first.

title: GeoAI Tutorial 1: Introducing the GeoAI Python Package
description: A new #GeoAI tutorial series where I'll guide you through using the powerful GeoAI Python package for integrating AI with geospatial data analysis and visualization.

title: How AI is Helping Transform Coral Reef Conservation
description: Pops, purrs and grunts - did you know a healthy coral reef emits a whole range of sounds? Marine biologist and data scientist, Ben Williams, shares how underwater audio recorders are helping scientists listen to the soundscape of coral reefs to measure their health. Yet, Ben quickly ran into an issue - there were thousands of hours of soundscapes to listen to! That’s where AI comes in. In a fraction of the time, AI tools can be used to analyse coral reef soundscapes to help track destructive human impacts and restoration efforts. This technology is a game changer for coral reef conservation and restoration - which couldn’t be more timely with coral reefs facing mass bleaching events and degradation.

title: AI Solutions for Climate Change: Jennifer Turliuk
description: Energy is the largest contributor to emissions by far, representing 62% of emissions (source: Project Drawdown). How can we reduce emissions from energy? We need to add more renewables like solar, wind, and so on - and we also need to switch to electric. But the electrical grid is a major bottleneck in getting there. What if we could leverage AI for energy management to reduce emissions?

title: AI in the Age of Climate Change
description: Nischal HP discusses the critical role of data credibility in combating greenwashing and enabling effective climate action. He shares how technology can be used to create verifiable data on carbon sequestration, empowering farmers and corporations to participate in carbon markets.

title: How AI Helps Us Track Methane From Space
description: Computer scientist Vít Růžička details how his team is using AI aboard a satellite to detect methane, a potent yet often underestimated greenhouse gas. With AI technology, methane detection might become more precise, helping science and industry accelerate efforts to curb emissions. Learn more about this new frontier of fighting climate change from space.

title: Green Software Ireland - Public Health Impact of Data Centres in Ireland
description: Conor Muldoon and Michael Bane's presentation on Quantifying the Public Health Impact of Data Centres in Ireland.

title: AI-Assisted Discoveries in Soil Carbon Cycle: Mechanisms and Predictions Toward Global Sustainability
description: This research leverages AI, big data, and mechanistic modeling to uncover how microbial activity and environmental factors shape soil carbon storage, aiming to develop innovative soil-based climate mitigation strategies.

title: Leveraging AI in ESG: Silver Bullet or Double-Edged Sword?
description: At the edie 25 conference in March 2025, the edie Extra stage hosted honest and thought-provoking conversations with climate leaders and sustainability experts about the ever-changing world of corporate climate action. Narrative Matters CEO Tom Idle speaks to Dexter Galvin, former CCO of CDP and current independent advisor, about whether AI can ever be a net gain for environmental sustainability.

title: Earth Species Project | Dr. Sara Keen | Breakthroughs in Bioacoustics
description: Sara Keen, Senior Research Scientist at Earth Species Project shares an in-depth look at how cutting-edge AI is unlocking the secrets of animal communication and paving the way for a renewed relationship between humans and the rest of nature. In this talk, she explores the progress made over the past year, highlighting major advancements like NatureLM-audio, a foundation model designed to accelerate bioacoustics research much like GPT-4 transformed human language processing. She presents a demo of the latest technology, illustrating how these tools can analyze vast amounts of animal vocalization data across species.

title: Can We Increase Sustainable Energy with AI?
description: AI can help us make more sustainable use of the electricity the world generates today. In the latest episode of ThePrompt with Trevor Noah, Dr. Jonathan Koomey explains how global internet use, which accounts for 5% of the world’s electricity consumption, is making the other 95% more efficient. Special edition: The Prompt with Trevor Noah

title: How to Harness A Great Untapped Energy Source
description: How can we minimise the energy waste from data centres? This week, Technology Now explores a potential avenue to harness a large untapped source of energy: excess heat. How much of a problem is the excess heat in the world? How much do data centres actually contribute to the problem? What, if anything, can we do to combat this? Malte Matthies, Business Development Lead for Modular Data Center - at Hewlett Packard Enterprise tells us more.

title: The Power Shift: Why Smart Energy is Smart Business
description: In the first part of our exciting new episode of Tamara Perspectives, Shruti Shibulal speaks with Arjun Gupta, CEO of Smart Joules, about his journey into the world of energy efficiency and the power of smart energy. Arjun shares how his passion for sustainability, combined with his engineering background, led him to create a company that’s revolutionizing energy use in India.

title: Why Investors Are Still Hesitant on Biodiversity
description: 🌍 How much is nature worth? According to the World Bank, nearly 50% of global GDP relies on nature, yet biodiversity and ecosystems continue to be degraded at an alarming rate. Meanwhile, the UN estimates that $8.1 trillion is needed by 2050 to address the biodiversity crisis. However, despite growing awareness, investment in nature remains slow.

title: Why DeepSeek AI Is Cheaper, Faster & Better Than the Competition
description: DeepSeek AI is unquestionably the media darling of the global information technology sector this year. News from the company in January about why DeepSeek is cheaper, faster and better at reasoning than other platforms roiled the NASDAQ technology stock market index and raised multiple questions, including: Will leading companies in the sector continue to increase their already frenzied build out of energy center capacity?

title: How AI and Accelerated Computing Are Driving Energy Efficiency
description: Josh Parker discusses NVIDIA's efforts with how AI is driving energy efficiency when it comes to computers as well as other applications.

title: Neutrons for Networks: Powering AI Sustainably with Nuclear Energy
description: How nuclear reactors can power the AI revolution, reliably, efficiently and sustainably with an amazing Ted Talk.

title: We're Wrong About AI
description: Will shares his untold story of broken promises and tough trade-offs at the intersection of AI, climate, and ethics. His talk reveals what it takes to confront corporate hypocrisy, challenge the high-carbon status quo, and align technology with climate science.

title: AI’s Hidden Climate Cost - How Much is Too Much?
description: How much water, energy, and carbon emissions does it take to train and run AI models like ChatGPT? And why aren’t the biggest AI companies making these numbers public? From the carbon emissions equivalent to round-trip flights to the lack of transparency from major AI companies, Dr. Sasha Luccioni and Brenda Wilkerson unpack the environmental consequences of AI and how it's much bigger than most realize.

title: AI for the Earth Digital Forum
description: How can we harness AI to protect, not harm, the planet? Join top experts and innovators as they explore AI-powered solutions for monitoring, protecting, and restoring landscapes, from satellite tracking to bioacoustics and beyond.

title: All Climate Solutions Exist, And Yet…
description: We have everything we need to manage climate change: the technology, labour, and capital. What’s lacking is the collective will. Julio Friedmann challenges us to rethink the role of developed nations, the moral compass of money, and the need for inclusive, cooperative frameworks to create an abundant and just future.

title: Atlantes: A Real-Time System for Global Maritime Behavior Analysis
description: Atlantes is a global-scale, real-time system that uses transformer-based models to classify vessel behavior from noisy GPS data, trained on 15 million annotated messages and currently deployed to analyze over 2.6 million daily trajectories for maritime intelligence.

title: This New Model can Accelerate the Energy Transition
description: The climate crisis demands urgent action, and while corporate investment in renewable energy has made progress, is it enough? We need to move beyond simply matching annual energy use with renewables and embrace a new model: the Clean Transition Tariff. This innovative approach fosters collaboration between corporations and utilities to accelerate the deployment of next-generation clean energy technologies.  By investing in solutions like advanced geothermal and nuclear power, we can build a reliable, carbon-free energy grid.

title: GreenOps in Practice: Building Sustainable Internal Platforms
description: In this insightful talk, Johannes (CTO at meshcloud) explores how platform engineering teams can take responsibility for their environmental impact. Learn how to build sustainable, resilient internal platforms and how GreenOps strategies help reduce cloud emissions while improving developer experience.

title: Towards the Age of Computation and AI for High Performance Climate
description: This talk explores how advances in AI and computation are transforming climate modeling, enabling higher-resolution simulations, improving data assimilation through machine learning, exposing current limitations in extreme weather prediction, and supporting efforts like the Swiss AI climate initiative to build more accurate, accessible forecasting tools.

title: AI for Climate and Nature Workshops Playlist
description: This video playlist features lightning talks from leading researchers, entrepreneurs, and decision-makers across AI, climate, and nature to explore opportunities for impact.

title: AI for Climate and Nature Spring Convening
description: This recorded livestream includes sessions from leaders within city government, academia, and frontline organizations on addressing climate and nature issues using modern AI.

title: Focus Area Spotlight: Sustainable Proteins // AI for Climate and Nature Grand Challenge
description: Dr. Andy Jarvis, Director of the Future of Food at the Bezos Earth Fund, joins Rebecca Meyer from Luminary Labs for a conversation on enhancing sustainable proteins and leveraging AI to amplify its impact.

title: Flexibility: The Key to Unlocking More Data Center Power
description: Are regional power grids actually constrained? Or could dozens of gigawatts of power be unlocked by new approaches to grid management and data center power contracts? That’s the intriguing question posed by new research from an energy analyst, and it could have large implications for the data center industry.

title: Can You Run a Grid Entirely on Renewables?
description: Can an electrical grid be run entirely on renewables, without facing black outs or wide spread power cuts? "Yes," says Anders Lindberg, President of Energy and Executive VP at Wärtsilä, on this week's episode of Cleaning Up. It'll just cost $65 trillion extra by 2050.

title: I Live 400 Yards From Mark Zuckerberg’s Massive Data Center
description: What's the true cost of the AI revolution and who should be paying for it? We went to Georgia to find out. Demand for data centers is booming as ChatGPT and other AI tools become integrated into our daily life. Under the Trump administration, investments into data centers in the U.S. is expected to surpass $1 trillion in the next five years.

title: Focus Area Spotlight: Biodiversity Conservation // AI for Climate and Nature Grand Challenge
description: Dr. Cristián Samper, Managing Director and Leader for Nature Solutions at the Bezos Earth Fund, joins Rebecca Meyer from Luminary Labs for a conversation on enhancing biodiversity conservation and leveraging AI to amplify its impact in the fight against climate change and nature loss.

title: The Growing Environmental Impact of AI Data Centers’ Energy Demands
description: The EPA has reportedly drafted a plan to eliminate all limits on greenhouse gas emissions from power plants, according to documents obtained by The New York Times. Now, with the rise of artificial intelligence technology, demand on power plants is increasing, in large part due to AI’s reliance on data centers. Ali Rogin speaks with Kenza Bryan, climate reporter for The Financial Times, for more.

title: We Went to the Town Elon Musk Is Poisoning
description:
Elon Musk’s massive xAI data center is poisoning Memphis. It's burning enough gas to power a small city, with no permits and no pollution controls.
Residents tell us they can’t breathe and they’re getting sicker.

title: How Can Climate Tech AI and Marketing Drive Real Measurable Change
description: I'm pleased to share our insightful conversation with Duygu Ozen, focusing on the hurdles climate tech startups face and how a conscious marketing approach can support their commercialisation.  We delve into the challenges of accessing finance and the importance of connecting startups with clients.

title: The Hidden Cost of AI: How Much Power is ChatGPT Really Using?
description: As Lionel built more interactive AI systems, he started asking a different kind of question. A big one. The kind of question that keeps engineers, artists, and policy makers awake at night. What the hell is all this costing us? The AI Energy Crisis Nobody Wants to Talk About. Most of us in the AI space love to geek out on what AI can do from generative art to automating workflows, to synthetic voices that sound eerily like your best friend. But very few people are asking what it’s costing us, in a very real, electrical, planetary sense.

title: The ESG Backlash Changed Sustainable Investing. Where Do We Go Next and Can AI Help?
description: Can AI help investors cut through the noise? And how can the industry rebuild trust in the value of sustainable investing? In this episode of Sustainability Wired, Clarity AI’s Chief Sustainability Officer, Lorenzo Saa, sits down with Alex Edmans, Professor of Finance at London Business School and author of Grow the Pie, to unpack the forces reshaping sustainable investing and what can be done about them.

title: An AI Engineer Is Here, and It Might Redesign the Physical World
description: Paul Eremenko from P-1 AI discusses a lot of topics with hot takes including data centers killing hydrogen and the Paris Climate Agreement being dead with the conversation shifting to climate change mitigation and resilience.

title: Bare Metal Documentary
description: Microsoft, Google, META, and AWS are some of the biggest single-power consumers in the world. Along with other digital infrastructure companies’ consumption, they make up 2.4% of the world's energy use. This film details the digital infrastructure industry's work to reduce their carbon footprint in an effort to lower carbon emissions and help save the world.

title: xAI Colossus Data Center Memphis Tennessee Video Recording
description: This optical gas imaging video of the xAI Colossus Data Center in Memphis, Tennessee was recorded by certified optical gas imaging thermographer Sharon Wilson using a Teledyne FLIR G620 optical gas imaging instrument.

title: Water and Artificial Intelligence (AI) for the Pacific Islands
description: Join us for a webinar about how water and AI are coming together for the Pacific Islands including the agritech community.

title: Sustainability - Doing Well While Doing Good
description: AI, data centers, devices, platforms… Our digital footprint is growing fast. But at what cost?

title: Before Rivers Rise | Using AI to Make Global Flood Forecasts
description: Floods impact millions of people worldwide every year, causing devastation to homes, communities, and livelihoods. But what if we could predict them earlier and more accurately through WeatherLabs, Google's newest AI tool for predicting extreme weather including cyclones.

title: Why Invest in Climate Change Startups?
description: “I’d really seen an industry come out of nowhere, right at the beginning in terms of the internet gaming industry, and that’s where climate tech is today” – We talk to Matthew Jellicoe of OnePlanetCapital about investing in early-stage climate tech funds.

title: EXCLAIM Symposium: Is AI the Future of Weather and Climate Modeling?
description: AI is clearly becoming increasingly essential - but not standalone - in advancing weather and climate modelling. Thank you to all the speakers for insightful discussions, and to the ARC Centre of Excellence for 21st Century Weather for supporting my participation. Special thanks to the organising committee for an excellent symposium!

title: Balancing AI Growth with Grid Flexibility: Inside EPRI’s DCFlex Initiative
description: The rapid growth of AI is propelling us into a new era of innovation that has the potential to transform daily life and drive economic growth. In this episode of the EPRI Current, host Samantha Gilman is joined by David Porter, EPRI's VP of Electrification and Sustainable Energy Strategy, and Briana Kobor, Head of Energy Market Innovation at Google. They discuss strategies to advance AI while minimizing costs and ensuring grid flexibility, featuring insights from DCFlex:  EPRI’s initiative to make data centers more flexible and grid-responsive. From demand flexibility to utility collaboration and scalable solutions, the conversation highlights cross-sector teamwork in advancing AI, grid reliability, and energy goals.

title: Can AI Be Green?
description: This conversation dives into the growing conversation around Green AI, the movement to make artificial intelligence more energy-efficient, transparent, and aligned with climate goals. Together, they explore the hidden environmental costs of AI, the race to build bigger models, and the groundbreaking work of projects like DeepSeek.

title: How do AI, ML, & DL Affect Global Warming?
description: A few podcasts on AI in Climate Science, Modeling and Predictions. AI is helping every sector with efficiency, emissions and innovations. AI can help monitor and reduce emissions in electricity production/transmission, agriculture, etc. But AI itself is an energy hog. Data centers are booming in number and size. Sustainability of AI itself is a major issue now. Market forces alone will not be. Enough to ensure this. Regulations and international coordination are needed.

title: AI and Sustainability Playlist
description: Dive into this groundbreaking podcast that unpacks the powerful intersection of AI, sustainability, and higher education. Listen to inspiring voices as they share how AI is driving sustainable development and reshaping education. Uncover the transformative potential of AI as well as ensuing risks and challenges in tackling climate change, addressing inequality, and powering smarter learning systems. Join the global conversation on the ethics, opportunities, and disruptions AI brings to education and its role in achieving the 2030 Agenda.

title: Sustain AI: Powering Sustainable Futures with AI
description: The full film celebrating the recent Bright Tide Sustain.AI accelerator programme in collaboration with Hogan Lovells (HL BaSE), featuring our Sustain.AI Ambassador, Lord Ranger, alongside several of the innovative ventures (Gamaya, Synature and Ecodetect) from the cohort. Sustain.AI is designed to empower the next generation of entrepreneurs harnessing artificial intelligence for positive environmental impact—from biodiversity monitoring and regenerative agriculture to climate resilience and nature restoration.

title: The Slow Death of Scaling and What Comes Next
description: In this keynote from Khipu 2025 in Santiago, Chile, AI researcher Sara Hooker examines the limitations of current scale-driven deep learning architectures and explores alternative approaches such as modular and thermodynamic models that could lead next-generation, climate-aware AI.

title: Saving the Planet with AI
description: Watch the keynote speech of Daniel Erasmus, CEO, ClimateGPT / Full Member Club of Rome about saving the planet potentially with AI.

title: Episode 5 - AI/ML are flipping Traditional Data-to-Theory Method to Accelerate Climate Change Understanding!
description: Watch this lecture of how AI and ML are flipping traditional data-to-theroy methods to accelerate our understanding of climate change systems.

title: Climate Conversations: Powering AI
description: Increasing investment in artificial intelligence (AI) is prompting a larger discussion around sustainability, from the huge demand for electricity to power data centers to the life cycle emissions of hardware needed to enable this technology. Globally, companies and governments are preparing for significant increases in energy use and raw material extraction to meet demand. On the other hand, evolving technology and breakthroughs in AI optimization could lead to lower emissions than previously anticipated. Join us for a discussion about opportunities to decarbonize AI and learn how leaders in the field are navigating the uncertainty.

title: How AI Uses Our Drinking Water - BBC World Service
description: More than a billion messages are sent to AI chatbots every day and each interaction uses water. Here, the BBC explains why AI uses water.

title: The Sustainability Attitude
description: Jamie and Ian discuss the intersection of cloud native practices and sustainability with guest Anne Currie. They explore the principles of building green software, the challenges of relying on renewable energy, and the importance of measuring the greenness of software systems.  The conversation emphasizes the need for practical steps to implement sustainability in data centers and the incentives for adopting cloud native practices. The episode concludes with a focus on continuous improvement and the importance of individual actions in driving change.

title: The Darker Side of Tech and ESG with Oliver Cronk - EP58: Tech World Human Skills Podcast
description: In this episode, Ben Pearce and Oliver Cronk delve into the often overlooked environmental impacts of technology. They discuss the lifecycle of tech products, the significant role of datacenters, the challenges of e-waste, and the energy demands of AI.

title: Green Software Engineering with Wilco at Voxxed Days Amsterdam
description: A conversation with Wilco Burggraaf, Principal Lead Green Software Engineering at High-Tech Innovators, exploring the practical side of green software engineering.

title: Efficiency and Rock n’ Roll (Really!) by Holly Cummins - JNation 2025 Keynote
description: Is efficiency rock and roll? You might think the answer is “no, definitely not, no way.” Efficiency is about doing more with less, and rock n’ roll is all about turning it up to eleven and doing more with more. Efficiency is about being responsible, and rock n’ roll is about being irresponsible. But wait! High overheads are not rock n roll, and they’re not efficient. Spending time on boring tasks is neither rock n roll, nor efficient. We’re spending way too much time writing and running code that doesn’t spark joy. In this talk, Holly will discuss practical patterns for wringing maximum value out of our code, for minimum effort. One of the techniques is sleeping! It turns out, both people and computers should be lazier, and that’s definitely rock n roll.

title: AI, Climate Change and Women Empowerment
description: Joyeeta Das is a prominent climate and deep-tech entrepreneur, currently serving as the CEO and Co-founder of Samudra Oceans, a London-based company focused on scaling seaweed farming using robotics and AI for carbon capture and ocean health. She is a serial entrepreneur with a strong background in technology and a focus on leveraging innovation for social good. Prior to Samudra Oceans, she founded GYANA, an organization democratizing AI with a no-code platform, and led SuperPitch to acquisition by Diversity X. Discuss how AI, climate change, and women empowerment can come together with this podcast episode.

title: 5 Minute Papers on AI for the Planet
description: Learn about how AI can be used to protect biodiversity, fight climate change, and just better understand our planet through 5-minute explainers covering academic papers on AI for the Planet. AI is not just chatbots! Grace Lindsay is a professor of Data Science & Psychology. She teaches a course on Machine Learning for Climate Change.

title: Is Whistleblowing Dead?
description: Featuring Zelda Perkins (Can’t Buy My Silence), Will Alpine (Microsoft Whistleblower), and Jennifer Gibson (Psst.org), the discussion explores the power and cost of speaking out - and why legal and cultural reform is urgently needed.

title: The Environmental Impacts of Generative AI
description: The popularity of artificial intelligence is skyrocketing, and so are concerns about its strain on the power grid and how it impacts the environment.

title: LSE Public Lecture Programme - Harnessing AI: Safeguarding High-Integrity Data for Climate Action
description: Artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) are versatile technologies that have drastically lowered the cost of data production and analysis, potentially supporting the acceleration of global decarbonisation efforts. However, concerns remain about their environmental and social impact, particularly the potential spread of low-quality information.

title: Urban AI and the Environment
description: As data centers expand across urban areas and public agencies increasingly adopt AI-driven tools, local governments face a pressing dilemma: how to harness AI’s transformative power without deepening its environmental and climate-related impacts. In this Urban AI Conversation, we discussed insights from the GovAI Coalition’s blog series on AI and The Environment, exploring how public sector actors can respond to these growing challenges.

title: The Energy and Environmental Impacts of AI Growth
description:
This is the second event in CURA, Sustainability Institute, and the Translational Analytics Data Institute's 2025 spring webinar series, "Is AI Sustainable?"
From your browser to your phone to your preferred source of news, artificial intelligence is seemingly unavoidable in 2025. However, in the midst of this explosion of activity, what are the hidden costs to this boom? Through conversation with leading experts, we will explore the environmental and energy impacts of AI growth, touching upon historical and future trends.

title: The Future of Clean Energy Procurement
description: This webinar covers the latest updates in global standards and regulations shaping the clean energy market.  EnergyTag is uniquely placed as the only organization directly involved in the updates of the main standards (GHG Protocol Scope 2, SBTi Net Zero, ISO) and leadership initiatives (e.g., Climate Group 24/7) that set the rules for clean power procurement.

title: Debriefing the Trump Administration's New AI Action Plan
description: Integrity Institute members Soribel Feliz, Theodora Skeadas, Leah Ferentinos, and David Evan Harris discussed the Trump Administration's new AI Action Plan. The new policy has sweeping implications for the way that AI systems are regulated and procured by the federal government.

title: AI Consumes A Lot of Water But Why?
description: We know that AI models require a lot of compute power and thus consume a lot of electricity, but have you ever considered the massive amounts of water consumption that AI requires? Check out this fascinating Ted Talk with Dr. Shaolei Ren.

title: Innovations That Matter By Deep Tech Startups
description: What are the cutting-edge innovations emerging from the National University of Singapore that are transforming the nation and potentially the world? Find out in this five-part series that showcases how NUS’ faculty, researchers and students are tackling pressing challenges such as ageing and sustainability, putting AI to work in the real world, nurturing startups and uplifting communities in and beyond Singapore.

title: Underwater Passive Acoustic Monitoring (UPAM) for Threatened Andean Water Frogs
description: In their WILDLABS Awards 2024 project, Mauricio Sebastián Akmentins, María Soledad Gastón, Martín Boullhesen, Ariel Prieto, and Yanina Soledad Palomas Alaniz developed the first underwater passive acoustic monitoring (UPAM) programme to assess the conservation status and long-term population trends of Andean water frogs in Argentina.  Over the course of their project period, they tested different underwater recording devices, species recognition software, and environmental sensors in order to create a standardized monitoring tool and protocol for surveying the threatened Pozuelos’ rusted frog (Telmatobius rubigo)

title: How Used EV Batteries Are Being Turned Into Data Centers To Power AI
description: As demand for energy skyrockets amid the rise of AI, one of Tesla’s co-founders is betting on a new solution: giving old EV batteries a second life. JB Straubel, who helped launch Tesla and served as its CTO until 2019, founded Redwood Materials in 2017 to recycle batteries and build a closed-loop supply chain for electric vehicles. Now, Straubel is using EV batteries that still hold usable capacity for grid-scale energy storage. Redwood’s new energy division recently partnered with AI infrastructure company Crusoe to launch its first microgrid, showcasing how repurposed batteries can help power data centers. CNBC visited Redwood’s Nevada operations to see how the company has grown and to learn more about its plans to use second-life batteries to meet the surging energy needs of the AI era.

title: Insurance That Adpats to Climate Change?
description: In this episode, we sit down with Nathaniel Manning, co-founder of Kettle, to explore how AI and parametric insurance are reshaping climate resilience. From predicting wildfire risk with 97% accuracy to supporting property owners dropped by traditional insurers, Kettle’s journey offers deep insights for founders, investors, and innovators.

title: Google's CSO on AI and Climate Moonshots | Climate Pioneers
description: Kate Brandt, Chief Sustainability Officer at Google, in conversation with Heather Clancy, VP Editor at Large at Trellis Group discuss climate moonshots and AI applications through Google's Accelerator Moonshot program.

title: The Environmental Consequences of Big Tech’s Push to Ease Regulations for AI Development
description: The rapid growth of artificial intelligence is creating a data center boom, but decades-old environmental protections are slowing efforts by big tech to build massive facilities. Wired Magazine has found that companies are asking the White House to ease those protections, and the Trump administration appears to be all in. Ali Rogin speaks with Wired senior reporter Molly Taft for more.

title: The Limits of AI Scaling Laws – NVIDIA CEO Explains
description: Jensen Huang, co-founder and CEO of NVIDIA—the world's most valuable company and the engine powering the AI computing revolution—explains the limits of AI scaling laws.

title: Inside the Town Where OpenAI Is Building Its Biggest Data Center
description: Abilene, Texas is home to 'the big one' as its mayor describes it. Here, OpenAI and Oracle have built out 8 massive data center buildings as part of the $500B Project Stargate endeavor signed off on by President Trump. Soon's Dagsen Love travels to Abilene to meet with small business owners, a radio DJ, the mayor, and more to find out how this town of 130,000 people is adjusting to an AI mega-structure being built in its community.

title: Goliath Has Fallen – The Audacious NIMBY: The Story of the Digital Gateway Project
description: What happens when ordinary people take on one of the most powerful industries in the world… and win? This is the untold story of the Digital Gateway in Prince William County—a project that would have become the largest data center complex on the planet. Backed by massive money, powerful interests, and behind-the-scenes deals, it seemed unstoppable, until the community fought back.
